title: fragaria nipponica var. orientalis
intro: Fragaria nipponica var. orientalis, also known as the Japanese strawberry tree, is a type of strawberry plant native to East Asia, characterized by its unique white or pink flowers and edible red fruits. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, due to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Fragaria nipponica var. orientalis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which may help protect against chronic diseases. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to promote overall well-being and support the body's natural defenses.
